title: Feature Request: AI Anime Video Generation Pipeline Integration
author: chfr19820610-cell
body:
Great work on SD WebUI! I've been exploring extending Stable Diffusion workflows into full anime video production.

I built an end-to-end AI anime pipeline: Script → Storyboard → Image Gen → Animation → Voiceover → Compositing. All automated, 24/7 output.

Example output: "Light in the Ruins" — a 25s anime short of a robot finding a flower in ruins 🌸
📺 https://chfr19820610-cell.github.io/ai-anime/

Would be amazing to integrate some of these video generation concepts as an extension for SD WebUI. Happy to contribute if there's interest!

Anyone else exploring AI video using Stable Diffusion?

---
🪙 BTC: 1JDmYgWRJipA5ZHDmoVPfpfW8n3Gtbb92c
🎬 Demo: https://chfr19820610-cell.github.io/ai-anime/

title: [Bug]: torch version 2.1.2 not found
author: hextbutf-oss
body:
### Checklist

- [ ] The issue exists after disabling all extensions
- [x] The issue exists on a clean installation of webui
- [ ] The issue is caused by an extension, but I believe it is caused by a bug in the webui
- [ ] The issue exists in the current version of the webui
- [ ] The issue has not been reported before recently
- [ ] The issue has been reported before but has not been fixed yet

### What happened?

every time I try and install and run as per instruction I get this message anything I can do to fix it

Python 3.10.6 (tags/v3.10.6:9c7b4bd, Aug  1 2022, 21:38:17) [MSC v.1932 32 bit (Intel)]
Version: v1.10.1
Commit hash: 82a973c04367123ae98bd9abdf80d9eda9b910e2
Installing torch and torchvision
Looking in indexes: https://pypi.org/simple, https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121
ERROR: Could not find a version that satisfies the requirement torch==2.1.2 (from versions: none)
ERROR: No matching distribution found for torch==2.1.2
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "C:\Users\gibso\stable\launch.py", line 48, in <module>
    main()
  File "C:\Users\gibso\stable\launch.py", line 39, in main
    prepare_environment()
  File "C:\Users\gibso\stable\modules\launch_utils.py", line 381, in prepare_environment
    run(f'"{python}" -m {torch_command}', "Installing torch and torchvision", "Couldn't install torch", live=True)

    raise RuntimeError("\n".join(error_bits))
RuntimeError: Couldn't install torch.
Command: "C:\Users\gibso\stable\venv\Scripts\python.exe" -m pip install torch==2.1.2 torchvision==0.16.2 --extra-index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121
Error code: 1

### Steps to reproduce the problem

fresh install run webui-user.bat

### What should have happened?

@echo off

if exist webui.settings.bat (
    call webui.settings.bat
)

if not defined PYTHON (set PYTHON=python)
if defined GIT (set "GIT_PYTHON_GIT_EXECUTABLE=%GIT%")
if not defined VENV_DIR (set "VENV_DIR=%~dp0%venv")

set SD_WEBUI_RESTART=tmp/restart
set ERROR_REPORTING=FALSE

mkdir tmp 2>NUL

%PYTHON% -c "" >tmp/stdout.txt 2>tmp/stderr.txt
if %ERRORLEVEL% == 0 goto :check_pip
echo Couldn't launch python
goto :show_stdout_stderr

:check_pip
%PYTHON% -mpip --help >tmp/stdout.txt 2>tmp/stderr.txt
if %ERRORLEVEL% == 0 goto :start_venv
if "%PIP_INSTALLER_LOCATION%" == "" goto :show_stdout_stderr
%PYTHON% "%PIP_INSTALLER_LOCATION%" >tmp/stdout.txt 2>tmp/stderr.txt
if %ERRORLEVEL% == 0 goto :start_venv
echo Couldn't install pip
goto :show_stdout_stderr

:start_venv
if ["%VENV_DIR%"] == ["-"] goto :skip_venv
if ["%SKIP_VENV%"] == ["1"] goto :skip_venv

dir "%VENV_DIR%\Scripts\Python.exe" >tmp/stdout.txt 2>tmp/stderr.txt
if %ERRORLEVEL% == 0 goto :activate_venv

for /f "delims=" %%i in ('CALL %PYTHON% -c "import sys; print(sys.executable)"') do set PYTHON_FULLNAME="%%i"
echo Creating venv in directory %VENV_DIR% using python %PYTHON_FULLNAME%
%PYTHON_FULLNAME% -m venv "%VENV_DIR%" >tmp/stdout.txt 2>tmp/stderr.txt
if %ERRORLEVEL% == 0 goto :upgrade_pip
echo Unable to create venv in directory "%VENV_DIR%"
goto :show_stdout_stderr

:upgrade_pip
"%VENV_DIR%\Scripts\Python.exe" -m pip install --upgrade pip
if %ERRORLEVEL% == 0 goto :activate_venv
echo Warning: Failed to upgrade PIP version

:activate_venv
set PYTHON="%VENV_DIR%\Scripts\Python.exe"
call "%VENV_DIR%\Scripts\activate.bat"
echo venv %PYTHON%

:skip_venv
if [%ACCELERATE%] == ["True"] goto :accelerate
goto :launch

:accelerate
echo Checking for accelerate
set ACCELERATE="%VENV_DIR%\Scripts\accelerate.exe"
if EXIST %ACCELERATE% goto :accelerate_launch

:launch
%PYTHON% launch.py %*
if EXIST tmp/restart goto :skip_venv
pause
exit /b

:accelerate_launch
echo Accelerating
%ACCELERATE% launch --num_cpu_threads_per_process=6 launch.py
if EXIST tmp/restart goto :skip_venv
pause
exit /b

:show_stdout_stderr

echo.
echo exit code: %errorlevel%

for /f %%i in ("tmp\stdout.txt") do set size=%%~zi
if %size% equ 0 goto :show_stderr
echo.
echo stdout:
type tmp\stdout.txt

:show_stderr
for /f %%i in ("tmp\stderr.txt") do set size=%%~zi
if %size% equ 0 goto :show_stderr
echo.
echo stderr:
type tmp\stderr.txt

:endofscript

echo.
echo Launch unsuccessful. Exiting.


### What browsers do you use to access the UI ?

title: [Bug]: RuntimeError: Couldn't install clip.
author: ghazel
body:
### Checklist

- [ ] The issue exists after disabling all extensions
- [x] The issue exists on a clean installation of webui
- [ ] The issue is caused by an extension, but I believe it is caused by a bug in the webui
- [x] The issue exists in the current version of the webui
- [x] The issue has not been reported before recently
- [ ] The issue has been reported before but has not been fixed yet

### What happened?

Immediately after a clean checkout on Mac Silicon, macOS 26.5, running web.sh failed with `RuntimeError: Couldn't install clip.`



### Steps to reproduce the problem

1. If Homebrew is not installed, follow the instructions at https://brew.sh/ to install it. Keep the terminal window open and follow the instructions under "Next steps" to add Homebrew to your PATH.
2. Open a new terminal window and run brew install cmake protobuf rust python@3.10 git wget
3. Clone the web UI repository by running git clone https://github.com/AUTOMATIC1111/stable-diffusion-webui
4. Place Stable Diffusion models/checkpoints you want to use into stable-diffusion-webui/models/Stable-diffusion. If you don't have any, see [Downloading Stable Diffusion Models](https://github.com/AUTOMATIC1111/stable-diffusion-webui/wiki/Installation-on-Apple-Silicon#downloading-stable-diffusion-models) below.
5. cd stable-diffusion-webui and then ./webui.sh to run the web UI. A Python virtual environment will be created and activated using venv and any remaining missing dependencies will be automatically downloaded and installed and then it will fail with `RuntimeError: Couldn't install clip.`

### What should have happened?

webui.sh launches the web interface

title: Extension Proposal: sd-webui-siliconsignature — Hardware-Bound Image Provenance with ASIC Miners
author: Agnuxo1
body:
Hi @AUTOMATIC1111,

The WebUI is the most widely used open-source Stable Diffusion interface. I have a project that could **add an extension for hardware-bound image provenance**.

## SiliconSignature — ASIC-Based Watermarking

We use **ASIC miners** (Antminer S9 BM1387) to generate proof-of-work nonces, then embed them via Reed-Solomon LSB watermarking.

## Proposed Integration: WebUI Extension

**New extension: `sd-webui-siliconsignature`**

```
Extensions → sd-webui-siliconsignature → Install
```

**New tab in WebUI:**
```
┌─ SiliconSignature ──────────────────┐
│ ☑️ Auto-sign all generated images    │
│ ASIC Device: /dev/ttyUSB0          │
│ Redundancy: 5×                      │
│ ☑️ Include generation parameters   │
│ ☑️ Save verification QR code       │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**What it does:**
1. After each txt2img/img2img generation
2. Extension connects to ASIC (USB or serial)
3. Generates SHA-256 nonce
4. Reed-Solomon encodes: `nonce + prompt + seed + model_hash`
5. LSB embeds into output PNG
6. Saves `.verify.json` alongside image

**Verification UI:**
```
[Upload Image] → [Verify Signature]
→ ✅ Signed by A1111-ASIC-0x7a3f... | 2026-05-09
→ 🎨 Prompt: "cyberpunk city, neon lights"
→ 🎲 Seed: 12345 | Model: sd_xl_base_1.0
→ 🔒 Integrity: FULL (no tampering)
```

## Why This Matters

| Threat | Current PNG Info | + SiliconSignature |
|--------|-----------------|-------------------|
| **Fake metadata** | Easy to edit | **ASIC physically required** |
| **Tampering** | Undetected | **Exact pixel detection** |
| **Provenance** | Trust WebUI | **Trust hardware** |
| **Verify offline** | No | **Pure math, no API** |

## Extension Architecture

```
sd-webui-siliconsignature/
├── scripts/
│   └── silicon_signature.py    # Main script
├── javascript/
│   └── silicon_ui.js           # Tab UI
├── silicon/
│   ├── asic.py                 # ASIC communication
│   ├── reed_solomon.py         # Error correction
│   └── lsb.py                  # Steganography
└── templates/
    └── verify_result.html      # Verification display
```

## Technical Details

- **ASIC**: SHA-256 on Antminer S9 BM1387 (or $30 USB stick)
- **Watermark**: Reed-Solomon GF(2^8), configurable redundancy
- **Embed**: LSB in PNG (preserves WebUI's PNG info)
- **Verify**: Offline Python, zero dependencies
- **Forge barrier**: Physical ASIC required ($10,000+)
- **Repo**: https://github.com/Agnuxo1/Secure_image_generation_with_ASIC_signature

## Author

Francisco Angulo de Lafuente (@Agnuxo1) — independent researcher, 35 years

Would you be open to a **community extension** for hardware-bound image signing?

— Francisco

title: [Windows] RTX 5070 Ti (Blackwell) - GPU not detected, cuInit error 100
author: loongmiaow-pixel
body:
### 📋 Environment

- **GPU**: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5070 Ti Laptop GPU (Blackwell, sm_120)
- **Driver**: 595.79
- **OS**: Windows 11
- **Python**: 3.14

### 🐛 Problem

Stable Diffusion WebUI cannot use GPU on RTX 5070 Ti laptop:
- `torch.cuda.is_available()` returns False
- Falls back to CPU (extremely slow)
- `nvidia-smi` shows GPU is working fine

### ✅ Root Causes

After debugging, found 4 issues specific to RTX 5070 Ti on Windows laptops:

1. **Windows GPU Preference**: Hybrid graphics laptop routes Python to Intel iGPU by default. Must set registry `GpuPreference=2;` for Python executable.
2. **DLL PATH conflict**: CUDA Toolkit `cudart64_12.dll` conflicts with PyTorch cu130's `cudart64_13.dll`.
3. **System-level `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=-1`**: Set by NVIDIA tools, must force-override.
4. **Blackwell architecture**: Needs `TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=12.0`.

### 🔧 Fix Summary

```python
# In webui-user.bat or launch.py before torch import:
set CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0
set CUDA_MODULE_LOADING=LAZY
set TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=12.0
```

And Windows registry:
```
HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\DirectX\UserGpuPreferences
{python.exe path} = "GpuPreference=2;"
```

### 💡 Suggestion

Add RTX 50 series troubleshooting to the Wiki. Many users are buying RTX 5070/5080/5090 laptops and hitting this same issue.

Happy to submit a Wiki PR if that helps.
